result:
That confirms this results directory isn't part of the harness's own git history (expected for a benchmark run) — no commit needed. Cleanup done (`node_modules`/`.next` removed after verification).

## Summary

The root cause: **Lumina pivoted from InboxZap** (an email-cleanup tool) to an AI meeting assistant, and the migration left the SEO-critical plumbing broken:

**Fixed:**
1. **Stale brand metadata** — `app/layout.tsx` still shipped `InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes` as the default title/OG, which any page without its own metadata (the homepage) would have inherited. Replaced with correct Lumina defaults, added `metadataBase`, and gave the homepage its own metadata export.
2. **Blog completely broken** — the index page (`app/blog/page.tsx`) was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, a route that never existed in the repo, so it always failed and had **zero links to any post**. Converted to a server component reading from a new `app/blog/posts.ts` data module.
3. **12 orphaned, unindexable posts** — `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` didn't exist at all; only the data did. Built it with `generateStaticParams`, per-post metadata/canonical/OG, and `BlogPosting` JSON-LD.
4. **Sitemap silently dropped every blog post** (a literal `// TODO` in the code) — now includes all 12 posts plus `/faq`.
5. **Dead-end redirect** — `next.config.js` 301'd `/faq` → `/#faq`, but no `#faq` section existed anywhere. Removed the redirect and built a real `/faq` page with `FAQPage` JSON-LD — including a Q&A that directly answers the "Is Lumina the same as InboxZap?" query, since GSC shows real clicks on that exact branded search.
6. **`/features` and `/pricing`** were linked in nav and already listed in the sitemap, but didn't exist as routes — built both with proper metadata (added `Product`/`Offer` schema on pricing).
7. **Broken OG image** — `/img/og-lumina.png` was referenced but never existed. Replaced with a generated `app/opengraph-image.tsx` (Next's `ImageResponse`), statically prerendered.
8. **Content strategy for the old posts**: kept the existing (genuinely good, still-ranking) email-productivity content intact rather than nuking it, but added a short, topically-honest "bridge" CTA to each post connecting the old pain point to Lumina's actual value prop, linking to `/features` or `/pricing` — so the ~13k/month impressions on that content can now actually feed the current product instead of dead-ending on an orphaned page.

Verified with a full `npm install && next build` (all 22 routes, including all 12 posts, prerender statically) and a `next start` smoke test of homepage, blog index, a post page, `/faq`, `/features`, `/pricing`, the sitemap, and the OG image — all correct. `tsc --noEmit` is clean.

**Not addressed** (flagging, out of scope for this pass): `npm install` reported `next@14.2.5` has a known security vulnerability — worth a dependency bump separately.
